key_points.text: Total revenue reached $215,938 million in fiscal 2026, representing 65% growth from $130,497 million in fiscal 2025, driven primarily by Data Center segment expansion.
Data Center revenue grew to $193,737 million in fiscal 2026 from $115,186 million in fiscal 2025, with Compute revenue reaching $162,361 million and Networking revenue reaching $31,376 million.
Operating income from Compute Networking segment reached $130,141 million in fiscal 2026, up from $82,875 million in fiscal 2025, representing a 57% increase.
Net income increased to $120,067 million in fiscal 2026 from $72,880 million in fiscal 2025, a 65% increase, with basic EPS of $4.93 versus $2.97 in the prior year.
NVIDIA powers over 78% of the global TOP500 supercomputers and supports 6,000 applications across enterprise software, autonomous driving, healthcare, financial services, and research domains.

citation.quote: Including GPUs and networking, NVIDIA powers over 78% of the supercomputers on the global TOP500 list, including 9 of the top 10 systems on the Green500 list. With support for 6,000 applications, NVIDIA computing enables some of the most promising areas of discovery

key_points.text: Gross profit expanded to $153,463 million in fiscal 2026 from $97,858 million in fiscal 2025, representing 71% gross margin, up from 75% in the prior year.

citation.quote: Gross profit 153,463   $ 97,858   $ 44,301   Operating expenses Research and development 18,497   $ 12,914

key_points.text: Operating cash flow increased to $102,718 million in fiscal 2026 from $64,089 million in fiscal 2025, demonstrating strong underlying business momentum despite higher inventory levels.

citation.quote: Net cash provided by operating activities 102,718   64,089   28,090

key_points.text: The company expects to commence data center leases with future obligations of $22.7 billion between fiscal 2027 and 2030, primarily to support research and development efforts.

citation.quote: Between fiscal years 2027 and 2030, we expect to commence leases with future obligations of $ 22.7 billion, primarily data center leases to support our research and development efforts

key_points.text: U.S.-based revenue grew to $149,617 million in fiscal 2026 from $77,482 million in fiscal 2025, reflecting 93% growth and increased U.S. focus driven by Compute Networking demand.
China-headquartered customer revenue declined to $19,677 million in fiscal 2026 from $25,048 million in fiscal 2025, reflecting regulatory export restrictions on H20 products.

citation.quote: In April 2025, the USG informed us that a license is required for exports of our H20 product into the China market. As a result of these requirements, we incurred a $4.5 billion charge in the first quarter of fiscal year 2026 associated with H20 for excess inventory and purchase obligations
